mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, as indicated by non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy and expanding economy. This boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is consistently positive (e.g., > 100k jobs/month) but SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is significantly elevated, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance. This typically dampens economic activity, increases borrowing costs for corporations, and reduces future earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 45% of periods when FEDFUNDS is above 5.0% over any 5-year rolling window, or if the average return is not statistically negative.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of robust retail sales and strong industrial production indicates broad-based economic strength, reflecting healthy consumer demand and productive capacity. This translates to higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND INDPRO growth > 0.3% MoM
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and INDPRO show positive growth as specified, but SPX 20d returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained rise in the unemployment rate, particularly during an economic downturn, signals deteriorating labor market conditions. This reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises as specified during a declared recession, but SPX 60d returns are positive in > 40% of such periods, or if the average return is not statistically negative.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in housing starts reflects underlying economic confidence, future construction activity, and wealth effects. This supports broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short, one-week horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows consistent positive MoM growth but SPX 5d returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power, increases input costs for businesses, and often prompts central bank tightening. This creates an unfavorable environment for equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 45% of periods when CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 4.0% over any 4-year rolling window, or if the average return is not statistically negative.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales and a low unemployment rate signifies robust consumer health and high spending capacity. This directly fuels corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows positive growth and UNRATE is below 5.0%, but SPX 60d returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, a significant rebound in real GDP growth from a low base signals improving fundamental economic conditions and renewed investor confidence, leading to strong positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized during a post-recession period, but SPX 20d returns are negative in > 40% of such periods, or if the average return is not statistically positive.
